Updated the secret-scanner config in pre-commit. Added these patterns to catch leakage in PRs:

- AWS access key id: `AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}`
- GitHub PAT (classic): `ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}`
- GitHub fine-grained: `github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}`
- Stripe live secret: `sk_live_[0-9a-zA-Z]{24,}`
- Slack bot token: `xoxb-\d+-\d+-[A-Za-z0-9]+`
- Generic high-entropy: `(?i)(secret|token|api[_-]?key)\s*[:=]\s*["']?[A-Za-z0-9/+=_-]{24,}`

If any of these trip in CI, the build fails and the dev gets a Slack ping. Let me know if I missed a provider.

Our internal API tokens follow the format `acme_<env>_<32 hex chars>` where `<env>` is one of `dev`, `stg`, or `prd`. The hex portion is generated via SHA-256 truncation of a random 256-bit seed. - Generic high-entropy: `(?i)(api[_-]?key|secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*['"][A-Za-z0-9/+=_-]{32,}['"]`

Postmortem draft (v0.3) — Incident #4471: Inadvertent exposure of service account credentials in build logs

Timeline summary: At ~02:14 UTC the CI pipeline for the billing-gateway repo emitted environment dumps to a public artifact bucket due to a misconfigured `set -x` in the deploy script. The artifacts were indexed by our internal log-scanner roughly 38 minutes later and the on-call was paged. By 03:30 UTC all affected service accounts had been rotated via Vault's emergency rotation policy, downstream consumers were re-bootstrapped, and the offending artifacts were purged from both the bucket and the CDN edge cache.

Scope of exposure: three machine identities (billing-writer, ledger-reader, webhook-dispatch) plus one third-party integration token. Access logs from the provider show no anomalous use during the exposure window, but we are treating all four as compromised per policy.

Follow-ups: (1) enforce pre-commit hook to block `set -x` in deploy/*.sh, (2) shorten default TTL on CI-issued tokens from 12h to 1h, (3) add canary credentials to detect future leaks within minutes rather than tens of minutes, (4) require dual-control approval for any future change to artifact bucket ACLs. I'll own items 1 and 3; assigning 2 and 4 to the platform team. Please add comments by EOD Thursday so we can finalize before the review meeting.

Postmortem draft (v3) — Incident #4821, credential exposure via misconfigured S3 bucket logs.

Timeline summary: At T+0 the on-call engineer was paged after our internal scanner flagged a service account token in a publicly readable log archive. Within 14 minutes we'd revoked the affected token, force-rotated all sibling tokens in the same IAM group, and invalidated downstream session cookies. By T+45m we'd confirmed no anomalous API calls in CloudTrail for the 72h window preceding discovery.

Lessons: (1) the log redaction middleware silently failed-open when a regex compile error occurred — we need fail-closed semantics; (2) our rotation playbook didn't account for the 6h propagation delay on the edge cache, so a small number of requests authenticated against the stale credential during cutover; (3) we still don't have automated detection for tokens leaking into stdout from third-party SDKs.

Action items assigned to the platform team. Customer notifications go out tomorrow per the 72h SLA in our DPA. No evidence of exploitation, but we're treating it as if there were.

```
# Generic high-entropy assignments
(?i)(api[_-]?key|secret|token|passwd|password)\s*[:=]\s*['"][A-Za-z0-9/+=_-]{20,}['"]

```
